jokes.text: MY FRIEND WAS WONDERING, UH... IF I GAVE YOU A JOINT, WOULD YOU HAVE SEX WITH ME?
jokes.setup: Awkward teen trying to proposition someone with the worst pickup line imaginable
jokes.punchline: The complete lack of smoothness and terrible logic of the offer
jokes.explanation: Classic awkward teen cringe that sets the tone for the show's realistic portrayal of high school social disasters

jokes.text: WE'RE GONNA BLOW THE ROOF OFF HIS GARAGE IN A MOST ROCK-TAGIOUS WAY
jokes.setup: Daniel trying to sound cool about their upcoming gig
jokes.punchline: The made-up word 'rock-tagious' reveals his trying-too-hard nature
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Daniel's wannabe rock star persona through his invented slang

jokes.text: MY DAD EVEN CALLS IT BETTY
jokes.setup: Lindsay explaining why they can't use her parents' car
jokes.punchline: The detail that her dad names the station wagon reveals his attachment
jokes.explanation: Efficient character detail that's both funny and reveals family dynamics

jokes.text: OH! SQUIRREL! Lindsay WHOA! JEEZ, MY NECK!
jokes.setup: Lindsay driving nervously with the freaks giving directions
jokes.punchline: She swerves dramatically to avoid a squirrel, jarring everyone
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy that establishes Lindsay as an inexperienced driver while building tension

jokes.text: JUST RUN OVER THE STUPID SQUIRREL, LINZE
jokes.setup: Lindsay swerving to avoid the squirrel
jokes.punchline: Kim's casual suggestion to just kill it, plus calling her 'Linze'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kim character moment - callous and mispronouncing Lindsay's name shows her dismissive attitude

jokes.text: LINDSAY, YOU ARE A TERRIBLE DRIVER
jokes.setup: Daniel talked Lindsay into stealing the car in the first place
jokes.punchline: Now he's criticizing her driving after getting her into this mess
jokes.explanation: Shows Daniel's self-centered nature - he creates the problem then blames others

jokes.text: HOW FAR IS THIS PLACE, JAPAN?
jokes.setup: Everyone giving conflicting directions while Lindsay drives around lost
jokes.punchline: Her sarcastic exaggeration about the distance
jokes.explanation: Basic frustrated driver humor, gets the job done but not special

jokes.text: YEAH. YEAH. ROLL DOWN THE WINDOWS 'CAUSE I GOT A BIG ONE A-BREWING
jokes.setup: Tension building in the car with everyone arguing about directions
jokes.punchline: Ken announces he's about to fart, making everything worse
jokes.explanation: Classic Ken gross-out humor that fits his character but relies on lowbrow shock value

jokes.text: THOSE MILLER FARTS ARE UNBELIEVABLE
jokes.setup: Ken farting in the car
jokes.punchline: He attributes the quality of his farts to the beer brand he drinks
jokes.explanation: Surprisingly specific brand name detail elevates basic fart humor into character comedy

jokes.text: OH, MY GOD.
jokes.setup: All the chaos in the car leading to this moment
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's horrified reaction as she presumably crashes
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the building tension - simple but effective dramatic punctuation

jokes.text: I HATE BEING IN SCHOOL AFTER IT'S OUT. MAKES ME FEEL LIKE A JANITOR
jokes.setup: Sam and friends in school after hours
jokes.punchline: His specific comparison to feeling like a janitor
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor that captures the weird feeling of being in school when you shouldn't be

jokes.text: JANITORS ARE COOL. I'D LIKE TO BE A JANITOR
jokes.setup: Sam complaining about feeling like a janitor
jokes.punchline: Bill earnestly thinks janitors are cool and wants to be one
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bill moment - his naive optimism and different perspective makes him endearing

jokes.text: WHY? SO YOU CAN SHOW UP WITH THE RED SAWDUST AFTER A KID THROWS UP?
jokes.setup: Bill saying he wants to be a janitor
jokes.punchline: Neal's specific, gross detail about the worst part of the job
jokes.explanation: Vivid, specific imagery that everyone can relate to from school experience

jokes.text: NO. 'CAUSE JANITORS MAKE WAY MORE THAN TEACHERS
jokes.setup: Neal pointing out the gross aspects of janitorial work
jokes.punchline: Bill's practical financial reasoning for wanting the job
jokes.explanation: Smart observation about economic realities that makes Bill seem surprisingly practical

jokes.text: BECAUSE I'M A GIRL
jokes.setup: Todd asking why she can't call guys
jokes.punchline: Her matter-of-fact statement about 1980s dating rules
jokes.explanation: Period-accurate gender role humor that establishes the 1980 setting

jokes.text: IT'S THE HAIR
jokes.setup: Sam asking what's so great about the guy she likes
jokes.punchline: Her shallow but honest admission that it's just his hair
jokes.explanation: Brutally honest teen attraction logic that deflates romantic notions

jokes.text: YEAH. ALL THE MEN MY MOM DATES HAVE FEATHERED HAIR
jokes.setup: Discussion about girls liking feathered hair
jokes.punchline: Todd's matter-of-fact observation about his mom's dating pattern
jokes.explanation: Awkward overshare that reveals family dynamics while supporting the hair theme

jokes.text: SHE SAID ANY GUY WITH FEATHERED HAIR IS FOXY
jokes.setup: Todd explaining his mom's preferences
jokes.punchline: Him quoting his mom calling men 'foxy' - awkward oversharing continues
jokes.explanation: More uncomfortable oversharing plus period slang 'foxy' adds to the cringe

jokes.text: THAT'S WOMEN. THEY GET TURNED ON BY WEIRD STUFF
jokes.setup: Sam asking why girls care about hair
jokes.punchline: Todd's overgeneralized teenage wisdom about women
jokes.explanation: Classic teenage boy pseudo-wisdom about the opposite sex

jokes.text: HEY, LADY, SHE SAID SHE WAS SORRY. YOU KNOW, WE'RE ALL A LITTLE UPSET HERE
jokes.setup: Daniel trying to be the voice of reason after the car accident
jokes.punchline: The irony that he's the one who caused this whole situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Daniel character moment - acting reasonable while being completely responsible for the mess

jokes.text: CALM THE HELL DOWN, ALL RIGHT?
jokes.setup: Daniel trying to de-escalate the situation
jokes.punchline: He immediately escalates by swearing and being confrontational
jokes.explanation: Shows Daniel's lack of self-awareness - trying to calm someone down by being aggressive

jokes.text: HEY, HEY, HEY, GUYS! TAKE IT EASY. IT'S BOTH YOUR FAULTS
jokes.setup: Lindsay and Kim fighting about whose fault the accident was
jokes.punchline: Daniel blames both of them while taking no responsibility himself
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Daniel character moment - the guy who started it all playing peacemaker while avoiding blame

jokes.text: HEY, LADY. THERE'S SOMETHING YOU MIGHT NOT HAVE HEARD OF. IT'S CALLED INSURANCE
jokes.setup: Angry car owner demanding payment for damages
jokes.punchline: Kim's condescending explanation of insurance to an adult
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kim sass - she's wrong but delivers it with such confidence it's funny

jokes.text: YEAH. WHO WANTS TO BE NEAR YOU ANYWAYS. YOU'RE TOO SEXY
jokes.setup: Angry woman telling Kim to stay away from her
jokes.punchline: Kim's sarcastic comeback calling the middle-aged woman 'too sexy'
jokes.explanation: Cutting sarcasm that's both mean and funny - peak Kim attitude in a crisis

jokes.text: I CAN SEND MY OWN DAUGHTER TO JAIL. YOU KNOW THAT?
jokes.setup: Harold furious about Lindsay stealing the car
jokes.punchline: The absurd threat to send his own daughter to prison
jokes.explanation: Shows Harold's overwrought parenting style - dramatic threats that reveal his panic

jokes.text: YOU'LL BE COMING HOME RIGHT AFTER SCHOOL AND SITTING IN YOUR ROOM. NO STEREO, NO RADIO, NO TV, NO NOTHING
jokes.setup: Harold listing Lindsay's punishment
jokes.punchline: The comically comprehensive list ending with 'no nothing'
jokes.explanation: Classic overwrought parent punishment speech - the redundancy makes it funny

jokes.text: DON'T WORRY. [SIGHS] I WON'T.
jokes.setup: Harold forbidding her from hanging out with the freaks ever again
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's resigned agreement suggests she's genuinely done with them
jokes.explanation: Subtle character moment showing Lindsay's genuine regret and disillusionment with the freaks

jokes.text: IF YOU WANT, I CAN GO GET MY UNO DECK
jokes.setup: Lindsay dealing with the trauma of the car accident
jokes.punchline: Millie's innocent offer of card games as comfort
jokes.explanation: Perfect Millie character moment - her sweet naivety in trying to help with something trivial

jokes.text: KATIE GOT SO NERVOUS WHEN SHE WENT UP TO DO HER ROUND THAT SHE GOT A BLOODY NOSE. AND THEN, SHE DIDN'T KNOW IT. SHE HAD TO SNEEZE, AND SHE BLEW BLOOD ALL OVER THE PLACE
jokes.setup: Millie trying to cheer Lindsay up with mathlete stories
jokes.punchline: The gross, vivid description of Katie's nosebleed disaster
jokes.explanation: Vivid physical comedy that contrasts the 'tame' mathlete world with Lindsay's current drama

jokes.text: WELL, NO, BUT... SHE WAS GONNA
jokes.setup: Lindsay questioning if Katie really fainted
jokes.punchline: Millie admitting she embellished but insisting it almost happened
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Millie's tendency to exaggerate for effect, then backtrack

jokes.text: OH, MAN. HOW AM I GONNA GET THIS ON WITHOUT MESSING UP MY HAIR?
jokes.setup: Sam trying to look good for school with his new feathered hair
jokes.punchline: The impossible task of putting on a shirt without ruining his hairstyle
jokes.explanation: Relatable teen vanity humor - the practical challenges of maintaining a hairstyle

jokes.text: BOY, I CAN JUST IMAGINE HOW MUCH THOSE PIRATES ARE GONNA CHARGE ME
jokes.setup: Harold talking about the body shop fixing the car
jokes.punchline: Calling the repair shop workers 'pirates' - colorful language for rip-off artists
jokes.explanation: Good Harold character moment - his colorful way of expressing frustration about costs

jokes.text: JUST PRETEND YOU KNOW A LOT ABOUT CARS. BUT I DON'T. THEY CAN SMELL IT, THE BLOODSUCKERS
jokes.setup: Harold giving Jean advice about dealing with mechanics
jokes.punchline: The escalation from 'pirates' to 'bloodsuckers' and the idea they can 'smell' ignorance
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of Harold's colorful metaphors for dishonest mechanics

jokes.text: SAMMY, YOU LOOK SO HANDSOME. JUST LIKE ONE OF THE HARDY BOYS
jokes.setup: Sam appearing dressed up with his new hairstyle
jokes.punchline: His mom comparing him to the wholesome boy detective characters
jokes.explanation: Perfect mom compliment that reveals generational gap - Hardy Boys aren't exactly cool

jokes.text: HA HA HA HA! WHAT, DID YOU JUST COME FROM CHURCH?
jokes.setup: Lindsay appearing more cleaned up than usual
jokes.punchline: Daniel mocking her for looking too respectable/wholesome
jokes.explanation: Basic Daniel mockery - fits his character but not particularly clever

jokes.text: NO. NOT AT ALL. HE WAS REALLY HAPPY THAT I STOLE HIS CAR AND SMASHED IT
jokes.setup: Daniel asking if her dad was mad about the accident
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's heavy sarcasm about her father's reaction
jokes.explanation: Good sarcasm that shows Lindsay's frustration while recapping the situation

jokes.text: MAYBE YOU'RE JUST ON YOUR PERIOD
jokes.setup: Lindsay angrily rejecting the freaks
jokes.punchline: Kim's crude, dismissive explanation for Lindsay's anger
jokes.explanation: Lazy gendered insult that fits Kim's character but relies on cheap shock value

jokes.text: YEAH, DANIEL, THAT'S EXACTLY IT. I'M ON MY PERIOD. YOU FIGURED IT OUT. IT WAS A JOKE. OH, SORRY. IT'S HARD TO PICK UP ON THE SUBTLETY OF YOUR WIT
jokes.setup: Daniel saying Kim's period comment was just a joke
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's biting sarcasm about the 'subtlety' of their humor
jokes.explanation: Excellent sarcastic takedown - Lindsay's intelligence shines through her anger

jokes.text: LOOK, I KNOW YOU DON'T CARE ABOUT BEING SMART OR GOING TO SCHOOL OR ANYTHING ELSE, BUT JUST BECAUSE YOUR LIVES ARE SUCH LOST CAUSES, DON'T KEEP ASSUMING THAT MINE IS
jokes.setup: Lindsay's mounting frustration with the freaks
jokes.punchline: Her brutal assessment that their lives are 'lost causes'
jokes.explanation: Devastating character moment - Lindsay finally articulating what she really thinks of them

jokes.text: I DON'T KNOW, BUT IT WAS PRETTY FUNNY. SHUT UP
jokes.setup: Kim asking what Lindsay's outburst was about
jokes.punchline: Daniel finding it amusing, then immediately getting defensive when called on it
jokes.explanation: Shows the freaks' defensive mechanism - deflecting criticism with dismissive humor

jokes.text: YOU KNOW, WHO ASKED HER TO HANG OUT WITH US ANYWAYS? LIKE WE NEED HER LITTLE JUDGMENTS ALL THE TIME
jokes.setup: Daniel defending himself after Lindsay's harsh words
jokes.punchline: The irony that he actively pursued her friendship earlier in the series
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - Daniel rewriting history to protect his ego

jokes.text: IT'S LIKE HANGING OUT WITH MY GRANDMA
jokes.setup: Kim expressing frustration with Lindsay
jokes.punchline: Comparing Lindsay to an elderly woman for being responsible/boring
jokes.explanation: Effective insult that captures how teens view responsible behavior as old/boring

jokes.text: I'M GONNA WAIT FOR MY DAD TO DIE SO I INHERIT HIS COMPANY. THEN I'M GONNA SELL IT AND MOVE TO HAWAII
jokes.setup: Nick asking Ken about his life plans
jokes.punchline: Ken's morbid but practical inheritance-based life plan
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy that's both shocking and weirdly logical - peak Ken character writing

jokes.text: I'M GONNA BE, LIKE, A LAWYER OR SOMETHING. I'M GONNA PUT THE POLE ON TRIAL. I'M GONNA GET GUYS OUT OF JAIL AND STUFF
jokes.setup: Nick explaining his career aspirations
jokes.punchline: 'Put the pole on trial' instead of 'people' - classic Nick confusion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Nick malapropism that shows his good intentions but poor execution

jokes.text: LINDSAY'S REALLY DEPRESSED THAT I BROKE UP WITH HER. SHE'S GOT TO GET OVER IT THOUGH, YOU KNOW?
jokes.setup: Nick explaining Lindsay's behavior
jokes.punchline: His complete misreading of the situation - thinking it's about their breakup
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy - Nick's narcissism makes him completely misread the situation

jokes.text: NO. I'M GONNA BE A DEEJAY, MAN. AND MAYBE, UM, A LUMBERJACK
jokes.setup: Nick being asked if he'll be a psychiatrist
jokes.punchline: His random combination of DJ and lumberjack as career goals
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully random Nick moment - the combination is so unexpected it's hilarious

jokes.text: THE NAME OF THE COMPANY IS TEXAS INSTRUMENTS, BUT I HEARD IT'S MADE IN TAIWAN
jokes.setup: Showing off a new calculator
jokes.punchline: The ironic observation about the company name vs. manufacturing location
jokes.explanation: Smart observational humor about corporate naming vs. reality - good nerd humor

jokes.text: HE SAYS EINSTEIN DID FINE WITHOUT ONE AND SO SHOULD I
jokes.setup: Lindsay explaining why her dad won't buy her a calculator
jokes.punchline: Harold's ridiculous comparison between Lindsay and Einstein
jokes.explanation: Perfect Harold parenting logic - absurdly high expectations with cheap justification

jokes.text: HE CALLED YOU THE HUMAN CALCULATOR. I THINK HE HAD A CRUSH ON YOU
jokes.setup: Talking about a judge at a math competition
jokes.punchline: Millie suggesting the middle-aged judge had a crush on teen Lindsay
jokes.explanation: Appropriately uncomfortable humor about inappropriate adult behavior toward teens

jokes.text: YOUR HAIR'S NOT LONG ENOUGH TO BE FEATHERED. I THINK IT LOOKS WEIRD, LIKE YOU'RE TRYING TO BE ALL FANCY OR SOMETHING
jokes.setup: Neal critiquing Sam's new hairstyle attempt
jokes.punchline: The specific criticism that he looks 'fancy' in a trying-too-hard way
jokes.explanation: Good friend critique - the word 'fancy' is perfectly dismissive for teenage boys

jokes.text: MY MOM ALWAYS SAYS THAT YOU SHOULD BE HAPPY WITH WHAT THE GOOD LORD GAVE YOU. WELL, THE GOOD LORD GAVE YOU A LOT, GORDON
jokes.setup: Bill quoting his mom's wisdom about accepting yourself
jokes.punchline: Neal's cutting response about Bill being overweight
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/punchline structure with Neal's quick wit turning Bill's wisdom against him

jokes.text: MY WHOLE FAMILY'S BIG-BONED. IT'S GENETIC. BESIDES, THE WORLD LOVES JOLLY FAT GUYS. BURL IVES, JACKIE GLEASON... SANTA CLAUS, UH, CURLY, RAYMOND BURR
jokes.setup: Bill defending himself after Neal's weight comment
jokes.punchline: His list of 'jolly fat guys' including fictional characters
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Bill defense mechanism - turning his insecurity into pop culture analysis

jokes.text: NO. RAYMOND BURR'S NOT JOLLY. WELL, HE WAS EXTREMELY NICE TO ME AT LAST YEAR'S AUTO SHOW
jokes.setup: Neal correcting Bill's list of jolly fat celebrities
jokes.punchline: Bill's personal anecdote about meeting Raymond Burr at an auto show
jokes.explanation: Unexpected and delightfully specific - the randomness of Raymond Burr at an auto show is perfect

jokes.text: TODAY IN CHEMISTRY, I SAT ON A PIECE OF CHOCOLATE AND HAD TO GO HOME AND CHANGE MY PANTS
jokes.setup: Cindy making casual conversation with the geeks
jokes.punchline: Her embarrassing story that could be misinterpreted
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - innocent story that sounds worse than it is

jokes.text: SAM, DID YOU WEAR A HAT TODAY? BECAUSE YOUR HAIR LOOKS KIND OF FLAT
jokes.setup: Sam trying to impress Cindy with his new hair
jokes.punchline: Cindy's innocent but devastating observation that it looks flat
jokes.explanation: Perfect undermining of Sam's efforts - Cindy's innocent honesty crushes his confidence

jokes.text: SAM, YOU LOOK LIKE YOUR MOTHER DRESSES YOU. HEY, WELL, AT LEAST I DON'T DRESS LIKE A VENTRILOQUIST'S DUMMY
jokes.setup: Neal criticizing Sam's style
jokes.punchline: Sam's specific comeback about Neal's formal style
jokes.explanation: Excellent specific insult - 'ventriloquist's dummy' is both accurate and cutting

jokes.text: LOOK AROUND. THE WHOLE SCHOOL DRESSES BETTER THAN YOU DO. CHANGE YOUR CLOTHES, CHANGE YOUR LIFE
jokes.setup: Neal defending his fashion sense
jokes.punchline: His dramatic life philosophy about clothing
jokes.explanation: Good Neal pomposity - turning fashion advice into life philosophy

jokes.text: WE LIVE RIGHT OUTSIDE DETROIT, AND THEY CAN'T FIND A FENDER? NOW I GET 2 WEEKS OF DRIVING AROUND TOWN LOOKING LIKE SOME KIND OF HILLBILLY
jokes.setup: Harold complaining about car repair delays
jokes.punchline: His dramatic concern about looking like a 'hillbilly' with a damaged car
jokes.explanation: Good Harold vanity moment - his pride in appearances despite practical problems

jokes.text: MOM! YOU ALWAYS GET ME SOMETHING STUPID, LIKE GARANIMALS OR SOMETHING LIKE THAT
jokes.setup: Jean offering to take Sam clothes shopping
jokes.punchline: Sam's reference to the kiddie clothing brand Garanimals
jokes.explanation: Good period reference - Garanimals were actual kid clothes with animal tags for matching

jokes.text: A MAN HAS TO LEARN TO DRESS HIMSELF. YOU KNOW, YOU GOT TO CUT THOSE APRON STRINGS SOMETIME
jokes.setup: Harold supporting Sam's independence in clothes shopping
jokes.punchline: His dramatic 'apron strings' metaphor for this minor independence
jokes.explanation: Classic Harold over-importance of minor events - turning shopping into a rite of passage

jokes.text: THIS IS THE MATHLETES, NOT THE FOOTBALL TEAM. / WHAT IS THE DIFFERENCE? IT'S STILL A TEAM
jokes.setup: Coach explaining mathlete fairness vs. competitive sports
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's logical challenge to the distinction
jokes.explanation: Smart humor - Lindsay's competitive logic challenging academic idealism about fairness vs. winning

jokes.text: YOU WANNA BE A STUD, OR DO YOU WANNA BE A SUPER STUD?
jokes.setup: Salesman asking Sam what kind of clothes he wants
jokes.punchline: The over-the-top sales pitch with 'super stud' as an option
jokes.explanation: Classic sleazy salesman pitch that preys on teenage insecurity

jokes.text: THIS IS A PARISIAN NIGHTSUIT, AND I PREDICT EVERY TRENDSETTING GUY IN THIS CITY'S GONNA OWN ONE
jokes.setup: Salesman showing Sam a questionable outfit
jokes.punchline: Calling a obvious jumpsuit a 'Parisian nightsuit' with fake European sophistication
jokes.explanation: Good con-man language - making something tacky sound sophisticated

jokes.text: BUY THIS GARMENT, AND I GUARANTEE YOU'LL BE PERCEIVED AS A MAN OF DISTINCTION BY THE LADIES
jokes.setup: Salesman continuing his pitch for the ridiculous outfit
jokes.punchline: The absurd guarantee about being perceived as distinguished
jokes.explanation: More sleazy sales language - the formal 'garment' and 'man of distinction' are perfectly overblown

jokes.text: YOU KNOW, I'M NOT A HANDSOME MAN, BUT I LEARNED HOW TO STYLE MY HAIR, STARTED WEARING SOME OF THESE CLOTHES. IT'S BEEN PRETTY COOL
jokes.setup: Salesman giving personal testimony about the clothes
jokes.punchline: His self-deprecating honesty undermines his own sales pitch
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - the salesman accidentally being honest about his looks

jokes.text: SHE REJOINS AND GETS INTO COMPETITION IN ONE DAY? THAT'S NOT FAIR!
jokes.setup: Lindsay being put back on the mathletes team immediately
jokes.punchline: Shelly's whiny complaint about fairness
jokes.explanation: Basic academic competition drama - Shelly's complaint fits her character but isn't special

jokes.text: THIS ISN'T THE LAST CHOPPER OUT OF SAIGON, SO CAN WE PLEASE JUST CRANK DOWN THE DRAMA A NOTCH, OK?
jokes.setup: The mathletes fighting about team positions
jokes.punchline: The teacher's Vietnam War reference to describe teen drama
jokes.explanation: Excellent adult perspective on teen drama - the Vietnam reference is both period-accurate and funny

jokes.text: IT'S THE NEW DISCO KEN DOLL! HEY, PULL THE STRING AND MAKE HIM TALK!
jokes.setup: Sam appearing at school in the ridiculous jumpsuit
jokes.punchline: Comparing him to a disco-era Ken doll with a pull string
jokes.explanation: Brilliant specific insult - the Ken doll comparison is both accurate and devastating

jokes.text: THAT'S A PARISIAN NIGHTSUIT, IN CASE YOU DIDN'T KNOW. A PARISIAN? OOH LA LA!
jokes.setup: Neal defending Sam's outfit with the salesman's terminology
jokes.punchline: The jocks' mocking French accent response
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows how the sales pitch sounds to everyone else

jokes.text: NO, NO. IT'S A JUMPSUIT. MY GRANDPA IN FORT LAUDERDALE WEARS THEM ALL THE TIME CAUSE HE'S TOO LAZY TO PUT ON PANTS
jokes.setup: The argument about what Sam's outfit actually is
jokes.punchline: Bill's matter-of-fact explanation connecting it to lazy elderly men
jokes.explanation: Perfectly deflating observation - Bill cuts through the pretense with brutal honesty

jokes.text: YEAH, I DIDN'T SAY YOU SHOULD DRESS LIKE EVEL KNIEVEL
jokes.setup: Sam blaming Neal for the clothing advice
jokes.punchline: Neal's specific comparison to the daredevil's jumpsuit style
jokes.explanation: Perfect pop culture reference - Evel Knievel's jumpsuits were exactly what Sam is wearing

jokes.text: I FEEL LIKE I'M IN THE SECRET SERVICE
jokes.setup: The geeks forming a protective formation around Sam
jokes.punchline: Bill's positive interpretation of their embarrassing situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bill optimism - finding the fun in a humiliating situation

jokes.text: NICE DUDS, JUNIOR
jokes.setup: Sam caught trying to leave school in his ridiculous outfit
jokes.punchline: The vice principal's sarcastic compliment using dated slang
jokes.explanation: Good authority figure sarcasm - 'duds' and 'junior' are perfectly condescending

jokes.text: NOW IF SAM WEARING SOMETHING DIFFERENT TO EXPRESS HIS INDIVIDUALITY MAKES HIM A 'HOMO,' WELL, THEN, I GUESS WE SHOULD ALL BE PROUD TO BE 'HOMOS.'
jokes.setup: Students mocking Sam with homophobic slurs
jokes.punchline: Teacher's well-meaning but awkward defense that backfires
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - teacher's progressive intentions make everything more awkward

jokes.text: THE, UH, THE MONSTER MANUAL. IT'S A DUNGEONS AND DRAGONS HANDBOOK. DO YOU PLAY? NO. YOU SHOULD. YOU'D MAKE A GOOD DUNGEON MASTER. I CAN TELL
jokes.setup: Daniel asking what the nerd is reading
jokes.punchline: Harris's assessment that Daniel would make a good Dungeon Master
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character interaction - the nerd seeing leadership potential in the burnout

jokes.text: NO, YOU'RE NOT A LOSER, 'CAUSE YOU HAVE SEX. BUT, IF YOU WEREN'T HAVING SEX, THEN WE COULD DEFINITELY DEBATE THE ISSUE
jokes.setup: Daniel asking if Harris thinks he's a loser
jokes.punchline: Harris's brutally logical analysis of Daniel's social status
jokes.explanation: Outstanding nerd logic - reducing social status to a simple equation about sex

jokes.text: TODAY IS THE FIRST DAY OF THE REST OF YOUR LIFE. SOMEONE SENT MY DAD A CARD WITH THAT ON IT FOR HIS 65TH BIRTHDAY
jokes.setup: Harris offering inspirational advice to Daniel
jokes.punchline: The deflating context of where he learned this wisdom
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of clichéd wisdom - Harris's matter-of-fact delivery makes it funnier

jokes.text: YOU KNOW, YOU'RE A PRETTY INTERESTING GUY, HARRIS. YEAH, I MEAN, YOU DO YOUR OWN THING. YOU'RE COMFORTABLE WITH YOURSELF. YOU GOT IT PRETTY FIGURED OUT. YEAH, I GUESS I DO. I DON'T HAVE SEX, THOUGH
jokes.setup: Daniel complimenting Harris on his self-confidence
jokes.punchline: Harris's matter-of-fact addendum about not having sex
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback and character moment - Harris's brutal honesty about his own status

jokes.text: 'SCUSE ME. AREN'T YOU DANNY TERRIO FROM DANCE FEVER? NO, I TOLD YOU, IT'S CHRISSY FROM THREE'S COMPANY
jokes.setup: Jocks continuing to mock Sam's jumpsuit
jokes.punchline: Escalating from male TV personalities to female characters
jokes.explanation: Good period references - Danny Terrio and Chrissy both fit the disco/jumpsuit era

jokes.text: ALAN, HOW MANY TIMES A DAY DO YOU HAVE TO REMIND YOURSELF THAT YOU'RE NOT GAY TO BE COOL?
jokes.setup: Alan bullying Sam with homophobic taunts
jokes.punchline: Mr. Rosso's psychological insight turned into a cutting question
jokes.explanation: Brilliant counselor move - turning the bully's insecurity back on them with psychology

jokes.text: SAM, WHEN I WAS ABOUT 20, I WAS HANGING OUT IN THIS HONKY-TONK DOWN SOUTH WHEN A BIG BUNCH OF REDNECKS SURROUNDED ME. STARTED MAKING JOKES ABOUT MY FRINGE VEST, MY HAIR. CALLING ME A HIPPIE, A WOMAN. THEY DRAGGED ME INTO AN ALLEY, MADE ME DANCE, TOLD ME TO BARK LIKE A DOG. DID YOU DO IT? YEAH. PRETTY MUCH HAD TO, THERE WAS 10 OF THEM
jokes.setup: Mr. Rosso sharing a personal story to help Sam
jokes.punchline: The disturbing details and his matter-of-fact admission he complied
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy that reveals character depth - the casual way he describes trauma is both funny and sad

jokes.text: WHAT MATTERS IS THAT I NEVER LOST PRIDE IN WHO I AM
jokes.setup: Mr. Rosso describing his humiliating experience
jokes.punchline: His inspirational conclusion after admitting he barked like a dog
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mr. Rosso moment - finding life lessons in complete humiliation

jokes.text: IF I SAY I'M THE COOLEST GUY IN THE WORLD AND I BELIEVE I'M THE COOLEST GUY IN THE WORLD, THEN SUDDENLY I BECOME THE COOLEST GUY IN THE WORLD
jokes.setup: Mr. Rosso explaining confidence to Sam
jokes.punchline: His new-age self-help philosophy delivered with hippie earnestness
jokes.explanation: Good Mr. Rosso character moment - his 1970s self-help philosophy is both sincere and dated

jokes.text: WELL, THEN TAKE A LOOK AT THIS KID. BECAUSE THAT'S A COOL KID
jokes.setup: Sam saying he's not cool
jokes.punchline: Mr. Rosso pointing to Sam in the ridiculous jumpsuit as an example of coolness
jokes.explanation: Sweet moment where Mr. Rosso's sincerity makes the ridiculous outfit seem genuinely cool

jokes.text: DAMN, I'VE NEVER FELT SO STUPID IN MY WHOLE LIFE
jokes.setup: Lindsay dominating the mathlete competition
jokes.punchline: Her feeling stupid while clearly being brilliant
jokes.explanation: Good ironic moment - Lindsay's academic success making her feel worse about herself

jokes.text: DID YOU GUYS SEE SHELLY WHEN SHE MISSED THAT SQUARE ROOT PROBLEM? SHE STARTED SWEATING LIKE A PIG
jokes.setup: Lindsay celebrating after the mathlete victory
jokes.punchline: Her mean-spirited observation about teammate Shelly's performance
jokes.explanation: Shows Lindsay's competitive streak turning cruel - she's becoming what she criticized

jokes.text: FORGET HER. SHE HAD IT COMING. I MEAN, I KIND OF FELT BAD FOR SHELLY, THOUGH
jokes.setup: Lindsay being cruel about Shelly's performance
jokes.punchline: Millie's conflicted response - agreeing but feeling guilty
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast - shows Millie's basic decency vs. Lindsay's growing competitiveness

jokes.text: YEAH, THAT'S WHAT MR. ROSSO SAID. YEAH, AND HE'S COOL. YEAH, BUT I ALREADY THINK I'M COOL. MAYBE THAT'S WHY NOBODY ELSE DOES. BECAUSE YOU'RE NOT. I'M REALLY COOL
jokes.setup: The geeks discussing Mr. Rosso's confidence advice
jokes.punchline: The circular logic ending with Neal's blunt assessment
jokes.explanation: Good geek logic loop that shows how self-help advice can backfire

jokes.text: I JUST CAN'T DO THIS. IT'S BEEN GREAT HANGING OUT WITH YOU, BUT IT'S JUST NOT WHERE I'M AT ANYMORE. THINGS ARE DIFFERENT NOW
jokes.setup: Lindsay leaving the mathlete celebration
jokes.punchline: Her breakup speech to Millie using relationship language
jokes.explanation: Poignant moment where Lindsay treats friendship like a romantic breakup

jokes.text: A FOREIGN FILM? IS THAT THE KIND YOU HAVE TO READ? WHAT'S THE POINT?
jokes.setup: Kim suggesting they see a foreign film
jokes.punchline: Daniel's dismissive attitude toward subtitled movies
jokes.explanation: Perfect Daniel anti-intellectualism - reducing foreign films to an inconvenience

jokes.text: THE POINT IS TRYING NOT TO BE A DUMB ASS FOR ONCE IN YOUR LIFE, THAT'S WHAT
jokes.setup: Daniel dismissing foreign films
jokes.punchline: Kim's blunt assessment of his intellectual aspirations
jokes.explanation: Good Kim sass - she's the only one who challenges Daniel's laziness directly

jokes.text: I JUST WANTED TO TELL YOU THAT I'M REALLY GLAD THAT YOU'RE FEELING BETTER ABOUT OUR BREAK-UP. I THINK YOU'RE HANDLING IT REALLY WELL
jokes.setup: Lindsay rejoining the freaks
jokes.punchline: Nick still thinking everything is about their breakup
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback showing Nick's continued narcissistic misreading of situations
